title: erigeron divergens var. nudiflorus
intro: Erigeron divergens var. nudiflorus, also known as Western daisy or western fleabane, is a perennial herb in the daisy family native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns. The medicinal properties of Erigeron divergens var. nudiflorus also make it beneficial for reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to harness its health benefits.
